# UC-22.8.22 · SOC 2 CC7 — Operational Anomaly Detection on Critical Batch and API SLOs

## Description

CC7 includes monitoring for anomalies. Statistical deviation on transaction volumes, error rates, or job durations for financially relevant pipelines shows detective monitoring beyond static thresholds—supporting availability and processing-integrity narratives.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes app and sourcetypes api:gateway, batch:summary. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Scope `service` to in-scope subsystems; (2) tune z-score window; (3) suppress known marketing events via lookup; (4) feed notable to on-call; (5) document model refresh cadence for auditors. Indexes required: `index=app`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=api:gateway`, `sourcetype=batch:summary`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) or MLTK.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=app` `sourcetype="api:gateway"` (service, status, latency_ms, _time); or `sourcetype="batch:summary"` (job_name, duration_sec, records_out).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Scope `service` to in-scope subsystems; (2) tune z-score window; (3) suppress known marketing events via lookup; (4) feed notable to on-call; (5) document model refresh cadence for auditors.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=app sourcetype="api:gateway" service="payments_api" earliest=-60d
| timechart span=1h perc95(latency_ms) as p95_lat, sum(eval(if(status>=500,1,0))) as err, count as vol
| eventstats median(vol) as med_vol, stdev(vol) as sd_vol
| eval z=if(sd_vol>0, (vol-med_vol)/sd_vol, 0)
| where abs(z)>4
| table _time, vol, med_vol, z, p95_lat, err
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC7 — Operational Anomaly Detection on Critical Batch and API SLOs** — CC7 includes monitoring for anomalies. Statistical deviation on transaction volumes, error rates, or job durations for financially relevant pipelines shows detective monitoring beyond static thresholds—supporting availability and processing-integrity narratives.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=app` `sourcetype="api:gateway"` (service, status, latency_ms, _time); or `sourcetype="batch:summary"` (job_name, duration_sec, records_out). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) or MLTK. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: app; **sourcetype**: api:gateway. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=app, sourcetype="api:gateway", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **z** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where abs(z)>4` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (vol with bands), Table (anomaly hours), Single value (count last 7d).

## SPL

```spl
index=app sourcetype="api:gateway" service="payments_api" earliest=-60d
| timechart span=1h perc95(latency_ms) as p95_lat, sum(eval(if(status>=500,1,0))) as err, count as vol
| eventstats median(vol) as med_vol, stdev(vol) as sd_vol
| eval z=if(sd_vol>0, (vol-med_vol)/sd_vol, 0)
| where abs(z)>4
| table _time, vol, med_vol, z, p95_lat, err
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Line chart (vol with bands), Table (anomaly hours), Single value (count last 7d).
